desc:
High in the sky, the world stretches out in all directions. The snow-clad
mountain tops, like white soldiers in formation, march on for leagues. Far in
the distance, the even greater heights of Caradhras can be seen. The wind is
cold and unforgiving.
name: Atop a Mountain Summit

desc:
View of the surrounding area is somewhat blocked by the rest of the mountain
ridge that is not scalable. This spot does offer some protection from the wind,
but slanted footing is the cost. The wind here is less harsh as the brunt of it
is shielded by the ridge.
name: Mountain Crest

desc:
A pair of mountains loom into the sky. Their icy stone slopes appear smooth and
featureless while at the crests, a circular cap of snow has come to rest. They
stand tall and proud, impervious to the passage of time. The snow becomes
increasingly deep to the west, having become caught between the two mountains.
name: Cleft in the Mountains

desc:
This deep ravine between the mountain slopes acts as a trough, catching the
endless amount of snow that slides down the slopes. The walls of the ravine are
steep and made of stone that is locked in permafrost. Any small sound creates a
magnifying echo as it bounces between the cliff-like walls.
name: Snow-filled Ravine

desc:
The mountains tower on either side, but here a small gap between them lies. The
lofty ridges up above make sharp outlines against the sky. Though lower in
altitude than the peaks surrounding this location, the snow is still plentiful.
name: Cleft in the Mountains

desc:
Like a giant half moon, the top of this mountain drops off into sheer cliffs on
one side. Ice has formed along the ridge making things smooth and slippery. A
lone tree stands near the top completely devoid of all greenery. The tree has
become completely frozen still and is as hard as a rock.
name: Mountain Crest

desc:
Snow, stone, and ice mingle together creating what appears to be a landslide
frozen in time. Debris can be spotted hidden in the ice and boulders of
considerable size seem to have been swept along as well. The air here is
motionless, almost as if holding its breath.
name: Frozen Landslide

desc:
This small valley hiding in the Misty Mountains offers a welcome respite from
the steep slopes all around. Bits of grass grow here and there on the valley
floor, mixed with stones and other rubble that has tumbled down from above. The
air here is calm, with almost no wind, and is shielded by the mountain wall.
name: Valley between Mountains

desc:
This valley is sandwiched between the ever climbing mountains. The valley floor
is covered in a thick layer of white snow. Though the area is mostly quiet, when
a sound occurs it is magnified and echoes throughout the landscape.
name: Valley Floor

desc:
The mountain is very steep and slanted here. Jagged rocks and heavy stones lose
their grip and slide down the mountainside at the slightest disturbance. The
clattering noise of their fall echoes in the mountains, caught and carried by
the wind.
name: Steep Slope

desc:
Two massive boulders stand here, looming overhead like the great snow giants of
tales. The boulders provide cover from the sun but the surrounding area is
mostly composed of ice save for slick patches of stone that appear suddenly in
the most unpredictable places. The air is still, but the frost in it lingers.
name: Frozen Ledge

desc:
Shimmering and beautiful, some crystals of ice hang precariously from the
ceiling; poised at any moment to crash to the ground. Icy mists lie like a thick
blanket over the ground. The occasional wailing of wind can just be heard
tearing over some opening somewhere in the surrounding caverns echoing off the
walls. A few large stones, which must have fallen from the walls long ago, now
lie on the ground covered in razorlike crystal ice structures.
name: Hall of Ice

desc:
Razor-sharp shards of ice shoot up from the floor and climb up the walls. A
frosty mist hangs over the floor, seemingly coming from cracks in the frozen
walls. The air is perfectly still, as if holding its breath in anticipation of
something.
name: Ice Shard Pit

desc:
Walls of chipped ice tower over the area. The ice is thick, with signs of
haphazard nicks and chips dotting the bottom. Clouds of vapor puff out of the
ground, covering the area in a thin mist. Failed attempts to scale the wall can
be seen, with frozen remains littering the floor.
name: Wall of Ice

desc:
The hard stone floor of this cavern is slick with ice. A long crack travels
along the walls circling the chamber. The cave's ceiling is quite lofty with
echoes getting lost in the darkness above. A layer of frost covers the stone
surfaces.
name: Cracked Cave

desc:
Though this cave is amply proportioned, it was made to seem smaller by the two
large stone platforms that occupy opposing walls. Each is over ten feet long,
and stands some five feet above the cave floor. It is obvious that they were
hewn from the chilly stone for some purpose, but what that might be would seem
unclear without considering the large, ragged remnants and poorly tanned skins
that cover each.
name: Frozen Chamber

desc:
Grey stones lie scattered all about the floor of this cavern. The centre of the
room is dominated by a single great rock with chisseled carvings that have been
cut into its face. The area surrouding the rock has been cleared of all rubble
to allow the carvings to be easily viewed.
name: Black Rock

desc:
A frozen altar stands in the centre of the room upon a dais of black obsidian.
Above the altar, a tall arch made of curved ice, so smooth that it could be
mistaken for glass, stands beautiful. Some bones and frozen furs have been
placed here as offerings to whatever deity the snow giants observe.
name: Icy Grotto

desc:
The walls of the chamber are made of frosted ice, forming an almost perfect
circle as they wrap around the chamber. Pillars of ice reach up from the ground
supporting the ceiling like trees of glass. Inside the pillars stand enormous
bodies of giants, their faces glaring out but frozen in an icy eternal sleep.
This isn't a cave, it's a tomb.
name: Circle of Pillars

desc:
Huge stalactites, made from pure ice, hang from the ceiling and stick out from
the walls like long knives. Several of the stalactites are missing, leaving only
their stumps behind, as if someone has ripped them from where they previously
hung. The walls of this chamber are made of stone but have long since become
encased within thick sheets of ice.
name: Ice Stalactite Cavern

desc:
A glistening sheet of ice blankets what was at one point a pond in the cave. Now
the ice is so thick and smooth it is like polished glass, reflecting any light
it catches. There are cracks snaking their way all through the walls creating
many shapes. A hole in the wall opens up to the sky.
name: Mirror of Ice

desc:
A thin, serpentine path snakes through the ice, with sharp icicles stabbing
through the walls. A thin path can be seen where countless footsteps have worn
the ice down to the shards of rock. The view of the ceiling is blocked by the
arching ice formations, almost as if the ice was the hand of a frozen giant.
name: Frozen Passage

desc:
Two tall pillars of stone stand at the back of the hall with a bear skin
stretched taut between them. A large wooden club lies propped up against one of
the pillars. Several large boulders with flattened tops are arranged in a circle
around the room.
name: Hall of the Giants

desc:
The chamber has been hewn from the mountain by living hands judging from the
smooth walls covered in frost. The floor is hard and frigid to the feet. Above,
pillars of ice descend from the ceiling making contact with the floor and taking
root. The walls are more rock here and show their age with visible lines of
sediment slashing across them.
name: Cavern of Ice Pillars

desc:
What appears to have been at first a planned extension of the tunnel is now
blocked completely with collapsed snow. The walls of this snowy crawlspace have
been almost completely buried by blocks of ice and snow which have fallen and
been crushed down by the weight of the glacier above.
name: Glacier Runoff

desc:
Dwarfing many of the surrounding mountain tops, this high terrace stands tall.
Rocks, large and small, are in abundance here and the wind howls as it passes
over them. The slightest physical disturbance often sends the rocks tumbling
down the mountainside, bouncing and crashing into anything in their path.
name: High Terrace

desc:
A great nest stands at the top of a high peak. Sheltered by a perfect alcove of
rock above and on one side, this is an excellent vantage point of the
surrounding areas. Ice creeps along the edges of the alcove but inside there is
no ice to be found.
name: Eagles' Loft

desc:
Slippery ice covers the slopes with scarce a foothold to be seen. Small stones
and pebbles have been frozen in the swirling white canvas. The world is split
asunder with one hemisphere a wall of ice, the other an endless sky. Far below,
the snow filled basins between the mountains twinkle and glisten.
name: Along a Mountain Crest

desc:
Along this mountain top a flattened natural path lies in contrast to the sharp
ridges that surround it. Narrow and with a cliff-like drop off on either side,
the path navigates over the mountain's peak. The wind sputters and whispers as
it races over the mountain.
name: Along a Mountain Crest

desc:
The mountain approaches its crest here but diverts into a small alcove that has
become a great eagle's sanctuary. The world stretches away below as the vantage
of this place is unparalleled. Large branches have been woven together tightly
forming huge nests with one particularly grand nest on a raised stone slab.
name: Eagle's Eyrie

desc:
The crest of this mountain climbs high into the sky giving an expansive view of
the surrounding areas. Snow has piled up layer upon layer and every so often
chunks around the edges fall off, crashing down the mountainside. The wind bites
the top of the mountain with no remorse.
name: Lofty Peak

desc:
The entirety of this mountain top is encased in heavy snow. The summit of the
mountain, approaching the clouds themselves, stands tall above the ground far
below. Great blocks of ice, like massive nails, surround the peak. The wind is
harsh and unforgivingly cold.
name: Snowy Summit

desc:
Nestled in the high Misty Mountains, this little dale lies quite hidden. Thick
grass lies underfoot with a small frost on top, giving the grass a bit of
crunch. There seem to be animal tracks imprinted in the grass, leading various
directions. Several moss-covered boulders lie close to the steep slopes that
lead back into the lofty mountains.
name: Grassy Mountain Dale

desc:
A small valley in the mountains lies here. Although the surrounding slopes are
covered in snow, here it's not so cold. A small spring can be spotted in the
corner of this little dale. Green grass and other plant life radiate out from
the spring, offering a welcome resting place to weary travellers.
name: Vale in the Misty Mountains

desc:
Towering above, the mountains wrap around this little valley like huge sleeping
bears. The almost permanent shadow that is cast on this land ensures the area is
not very warm. A small pond, frozen over with a thin sheet of ice, lies
motionless in the corner of the valley.
name: In the Shadow of the Misty Mountains
